{{Infobox royalty | name = 豊城入彦命 | Name in Various Languages = | Family Name/Title = | Image = | Image Size = | Image Description = | Relationship = 第10代{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}{{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} | Title = | Full Name = | Rank = | Honorific = | Seal/Emblem = | Date of Birth = | Place of Birth = | Date of Death = | Place of Death = | Burial Date = | Burial Place = | Spouse 1 = | issue = {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田">『新撰姓氏録』皇別 和泉国 登美首条等。</ref> | father = {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}} | Position/Office = }}
'''Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto''' (豊城入彦命) was an ancient Japanese {{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
He was a son of the 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} and is believed to be the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} refer to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto" or "Toyoki no Mikoto," while the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} refers to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto."
== Genealogy ==
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. His mother was {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}}, the daughter of Arakawatobime. He was the elder half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} and the full brother of {{Ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}.
== Records ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The {{ill|Futagoyama Kofun|ja|総社二子山古墳}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}}, {{ill|Kogabikikami|ja|鹿我別}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, and many others listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}.
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}.

=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, and many others listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}.
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}.

{{Infobox royalty | name = 豊城入彦命 | Name in Various Languages = | Family Name/Title = | Image = | Image Size = | Image Description = | Relationship = 第10代{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}{{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} | Title = | Full Name = | Rank = | Honorific = | Seal/Emblem = | Date of Birth = | Place of Birth = | Date of Death = | Place of Death = | Burial Date = | Burial Place = | Spouse 1 = | issue = {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田">『新撰姓氏録』皇別 和泉国 登美首条等。</ref> | father = {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}} | Position/Office = }}
'''Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto''' (豊城入彦命) was an ancient Japanese {{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
He was a son of the 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} and is believed to be the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} refer to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto" or "Toyoki no Mikoto," while the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} refers to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto."
== Genealogy ==
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. His mother was {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}}, the daughter of Arakawatobime. He was the elder half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} and the full brother of {{Ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}.
== Records ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The {{ill|Futagoyama Kofun|ja|総社二子山古墳}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}}, {{ill|Kogabikikami|ja|鹿我別}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, and many others listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}.
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}.

'''Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto''' (豊城入彦命) was an ancient Japanese {{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
He was a son of the 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} and is believed to be the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} refer to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto" or "Toyoki no Mikoto," while the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} refers to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto."
== Genealogy ==
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. His mother was {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}}, the daughter of Arakawatobime. He was the elder half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} and the full brother of {{Ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}.
== Records ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The {{ill|Futagoyama Kofun|ja|総社二子山古墳}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}}, {{ill|Kogabikikami|ja|鹿我別}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, and many others listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}.
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}.

revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = 豊城入彦命 | Name in Various Languages = | Family Name/Title = | Image = | Image Size = | Image Description = | Relationship = 第10代{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}{{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} | Title = | Full Name = | Rank = | Honorific = | Seal/Emblem = | Date of Birth = | Place of Birth = | Date of Death = | Place of Death = | Burial Date = | Burial Place = | Spouse 1 = | issue = {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田">『新撰姓氏録』皇別 和泉国 登美首条等。</ref> | father = {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}} | Position/Office = }}
'''Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto''' (豊城入彦命) was an ancient Japanese {{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
He was a son of the 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} and is believed to be the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} refer to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto" or "Toyoki no Mikoto," while the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} refers to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto."
== Genealogy ==
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. His mother was {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}}, the daughter of Arakawatobime. He was the elder half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} and the full brother of {{Ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}.
== Records ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The {{ill|Futagoyama Kofun|ja|総社二子山古墳}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}}, {{ill|Kogabikikami|ja|鹿我別}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, and many others listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}.
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}.
== Notes == {{Notelist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}}
== References == {{reflist|group="原"}} {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|author=吉川弘文館|year=2010|chapter=豊城入彦命|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|isbn=978-4642014588}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futaarayama Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}

{{Infobox royalty | name = 豊城入彦命 | Name in Various Languages = | Family Name/Title = | Image = | Image Size = | Image Description = | Relationship = 第10代{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}{{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} | Title = | Full Name = | Rank = | Honorific = | Seal/Emblem = | Date of Birth = | Place of Birth = | Date of Death = | Place of Death = | Burial Date = | Burial Place = | Spouse 1 = | issue = {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田">『新撰姓氏録』皇別 和泉国 登美首条等。</ref> | father = {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}} | Position/Office = }}
'''Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto''' (豊城入彦命) was an ancient Japanese {{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
He was a son of the 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} and is believed to be the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} refer to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto" or "Toyoki no Mikoto," while the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} refers to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto."
== Genealogy ==
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. His mother was {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}}, the daughter of Arakawatobime. He was the elder half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} and the full brother of {{Ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}.
== Records ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The {{ill|Futagoyama Kofun|ja|総社二子山古墳}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}}, {{ill|Kogabikikami|ja|鹿我別}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, and many others listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}.
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}.

Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍}} expeditions. {{Infobox royalty | name = 豊城入彦命 | Name in Various Languages = | Family Name/Title = | Image = | Image Size = | Image Description = | Relationship = 第10代{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}{{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} | Title = | Full Name = | Rank = | Honorific = | Seal/Emblem = | Date of Birth = | Place of Birth = | Date of Death = | Place of Death = | Burial Date = | Burial Place = | Spouse 1 = | issue = {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田">『新撰姓氏録』皇別 和泉国 登美首条等。</ref> | father = {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}} | Position/Office = }}
'''Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto''' (豊城入彦命) was an ancient Japanese {{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
He was a son of the 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} and is believed to be the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} refer to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto" or "Toyoki no Mikoto," while the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} refers to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto."
== Genealogy ==
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. His mother was {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}}, the daughter of Arakawatobime. He was the elder half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} and the full brother of {{Ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}.
== Records ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The {{ill|Futagoyama Kofun|ja|総社二子山古墳}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}}, {{ill|Kogabikikami|ja|鹿我別}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, and many others listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}.
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}.

revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = 豊城入彦命 | Name in Various Languages = | Family Name/Title = | Image = | Image Size = | Image Description = | Relationship = 第10代{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}{{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} | Title = | Full Name = | Rank = | Honorific = | Seal/Emblem = | Date of Birth = | Place of Birth = | Date of Death = | Place of Death = | Burial Date = | Burial Place = | Spouse 1 = | issue = {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田">『新撰姓氏録』皇別 和泉国 登美首条等。</ref> | father = {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}} | Position/Office = }}
'''Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto''' (豊城入彦命) was an ancient Japanese {{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
He was a son of the 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} and is believed to be the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} refer to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto" or "Toyoki no Mikoto," while the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} refers to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto."
== Genealogy ==
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. His mother was {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}}, the daughter of Arakawatobime. He was the elder half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} and the full brother of {{Ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}.
== Records ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The {{ill|Futagoyama Kofun|ja|総社二子山古墳}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}}, {{ill|Kogabikikami|ja|鹿我別}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, and many others listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}.
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}.
== Notes == {{Notelist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}}
== References == {{reflist|group="原"}} {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|author=吉川弘文館|year=2010|chapter=豊城入彦命|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|isbn=978-4642014588}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futaarayama Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = 豊城入彦命 | Name in Various Languages = | Family Name/Title = | Image = | Image Size = | Image Description = | Relationship = 第10代{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}{{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} | Title = | Full Name = | Rank = | Honorific = | Seal/Emblem = | Date of Birth = | Place of Birth = | Date of Death = | Place of Death = | Burial Date = | Burial Place = | Spouse 1 = | issue = {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田">『新撰姓氏録』皇別 和泉国 登美首条等。</ref> | father = {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}} | Position/Office = }}
'''Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto''' (豊城入彦命) was an ancient Japanese {{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
He was a son of the 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} and is believed to be the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} refer to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto" or "Toyoki no Mikoto," while the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} refers to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto."
== Genealogy ==
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. His mother was {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}}, the daughter of Arakawatobime. He was the elder half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} and the full brother of {{Ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}.
== Records ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The {{ill|Futagoyama Kofun|ja|総社二子山古墳}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, and many others listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}.
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}.
== Notes == {{Notelist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}}
== References == {{reflist|group="原"}} {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|author=吉川弘文館|year=2010|chapter=豊城入彦命|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|isbn=978-4642014588}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futaarayama Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}

{{Infobox royalty | name = 豊城入彦命 | Name in Various Languages = | Family Name/Title = | Image = | Image Size = | Image Description = | Relationship = 第10代{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}{{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} | Title = | Full Name = | Rank = | Honorific = | Seal/Emblem = | Date of Birth = | Place of Birth = | Date of Death = | Place of Death = | Burial Date = | Burial Place = | Spouse 1 = | issue = {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田">『新撰姓氏録』皇別 和泉国 登美首条等。</ref> | father = {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}} | Position/Office = }}
'''Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto''' (豊城入彦命) was an ancient Japanese {{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
He was a son of the 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} and is believed to be the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} refer to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto" or "Toyoki no Mikoto," while the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} refers to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto."
== Genealogy ==
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. His mother was {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}}, the daughter of Arakawatobime. He was the elder half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} and the full brother of {{Ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}.
== Records ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The {{ill|Futagoyama Kofun|ja|総社二子山古墳}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, and many others listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}.
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}.

{{Infobox royalty | name = 豊城入彦命 | Name in Various Languages = | Family Name/Title = | Image = | Image Size = | Image Description = | Relationship = 第10代{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}{{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} | Title = | Full Name = | Rank = | Honorific = | Seal/Emblem = | Date of Birth = | Place of Birth = | Date of Death = | Place of Death = | Burial Date = | Burial Place = | Spouse 1 = | issue = {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田">『新撰姓氏録』皇別 和泉国 登美首条等。</ref> | father = {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}} | Position/Office = }}
'''Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto''' (豊城入彦命) was an ancient Japanese {{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
He was a son of the 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} and is believed to be the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} refer to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto" or "Toyoki no Mikoto," while the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} refers to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto."
== Genealogy ==
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. His mother was {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}}, the daughter of Arakawatobime. He was the elder half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} and the full brother of {{Ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}.
== Records ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The {{ill|Futagoyama Kofun|ja|総社二子山古墳}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, and many others listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}.
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}.

{{Infobox royalty | name = 豊城入彦命 | Name in Various Languages = | Family Name/Title = | Image = | Image Size = | Image Description = | Relationship = 第10代{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}{{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} | Title = | Full Name = | Rank = | Honorific = | Seal/Emblem = | Date of Birth = | Place of Birth = | Date of Death = | Place of Death = | Burial Date = | Burial Place = | Spouse 1 = | issue = {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田">『新撰姓氏録』皇別 和泉国 登美首条等。</ref> | father = {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}} | Position/Office = }}
'''Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto''' (豊城入彦命) was an ancient Japanese {{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
He was a son of the 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} and is believed to be the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} refer to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto" or "Toyoki no Mikoto," while the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} refers to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto."
== Genealogy ==
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. His mother was {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}}, the daughter of Arakawatobime. He was the elder half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} and the full brother of {{Ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}.
== Records ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The {{ill|Futagoyama Kofun|ja|総社二子山古墳}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, and many others listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}.
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}.

The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}.

{{Infobox royalty | name = 豊城入彦命 | Name in Various Languages = | Family Name/Title = | Image = | Image Size = | Image Description = | Relationship = 第10代{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}{{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} | Title = | Full Name = | Rank = | Honorific = | Seal/Emblem = | Date of Birth = | Place of Birth = | Date of Death = | Place of Death = | Burial Date = | Burial Place = | Spouse 1 = | issue = {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田">『新撰姓氏録』皇別 和泉国 登美首条等。</ref> | father = {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}} | Position/Office = }}
'''Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto''' (豊城入彦命) was an ancient Japanese {{ill|Prince (son of Emperor)|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
He was a son of the 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} and is believed to be the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} refer to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto" or "Toyoki no Mikoto," while the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} refers to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto."
== Genealogy ==
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. His mother was {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}}, the daughter of Arakawatobime. He was the elder half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} and the full brother of {{Ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}.
== Records ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The {{ill|Futagoyama Kofun|ja|総社二子山古墳}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, and many others listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}.
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}.

=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, and many others listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}.
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}.

== References == {{reflist|group="原"}} {{reflist}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox royalty | name = 豊城入彦命 | Name in Various Languages = | Family Name/Title = | Image = | Image Size = | Image Description = | Relationship = 第10代{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} | Title = | Full Name = | Rank = | Honorific = | Seal/Emblem = | Date of Birth = | Place of Birth = | Date of Death = | Place of Death = | Burial Date = | Burial Place = | Spouse 1 = | issue = {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田">『新撰姓氏録』皇別 和泉国 登美首条等。</ref> | father = {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}} | Position/Office = }}
'''Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto''' (豊城入彦命) was an ancient Japanese {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
He was a son of the 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} and is believed to be the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} refer to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto" or "Toyoki no Mikoto," while the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} refers to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto."
== Genealogy ==
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. His mother was {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}}, the daughter of Arakawatobime. He was the elder half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} and the full brother of {{Ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}.
== Records ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The {{ill|Futagoyama Kofun|ja|総社二子山古墳}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, and many others listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}.
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}.
== Notes == {{Notelist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}}
== References == {{reflist|group="原"}} {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|author=吉川弘文館|year=2010|chapter=豊城入彦命|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|isbn=978-4642014588}}

Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, and many others listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}.
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}.

{{Infobox royalty | name = 豊城入彦命 | Name in Various Languages = | Family Name/Title = | Image = | Image Size = | Image Description = | Relationship = 第10代{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} | Title = | Full Name = | Rank = | Honorific = | Seal/Emblem = | Date of Birth = | Place of Birth = | Date of Death = | Place of Death = | Burial Date = | Burial Place = | Spouse 1 = | issue = {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田">『新撰姓氏録』皇別 和泉国 登美首条等。</ref> | father = {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}} | Position/Office = }}
'''Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto''' (豊城入彦命) was an ancient Japanese {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
He was a son of the 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} and is believed to be the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} refer to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto" or "Toyoki no Mikoto," while the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} refers to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto."
== Genealogy ==
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. His mother was {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}}, the daughter of Arakawatobime. He was the elder half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} and the full brother of {{Ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}.
== Records ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The {{ill|Futagoyama Kofun|ja|総社二子山古墳}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, and many others listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}.
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}.

== Records ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The {{ill|Futagoyama Kofun|ja|総社二子山古墳}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, and many others listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}.
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}.

{{Infobox royalty | name = 豊城入彦命 | Name in Various Languages = | Family Name/Title = | Image = | Image Size = | Image Description = | Relationship = 第10代{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} | Title = | Full Name = | Rank = | Honorific = | Seal/Emblem = | Date of Birth = | Place of Birth = | Date of Death = | Place of Death = | Burial Date = | Burial Place = | Spouse 1 = | issue = {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田">『新撰姓氏録』皇別 和泉国 登美首条等。</ref> | father = {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}} | Position/Office = }}
'''Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto''' (豊城入彦命) was an ancient Japanese {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
He was a son of the 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} and is believed to be the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} refer to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto" or "Toyoki no Mikoto," while the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} refers to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto."
== Genealogy ==
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. His mother was {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}}, the daughter of Arakawatobime. He was the elder half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} and the full brother of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}.
== Records ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The {{ill|Futagoyama Kofun|ja|総社二子山古墳}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.

=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, and many others listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}.
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}.

== Genealogy ==
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. His mother was {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}}, the daughter of Arakawatobime. He was the elder half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} and the full brother of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}.
== Records ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The {{ill|Futagoyama Kofun|ja|総社二子山古墳}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, and many others listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}.
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}.

== References == {{reflist|group="原"}} {{reflist}}

{{Infobox royalty | name = 豊城入彦命 | Name in Various Languages = | Family Name/Title = | image = | image Size = | image Description = | Relationship = 第10代{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} | Title = | Full Name = | Rank = | Honorific = | Seal/Emblem = | Date of Birth = | Place of Birth = | Date of Death = | Place of Death = | Burial Date = | Burial Place = | Spouse 1 = | issue = {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田">『新撰姓氏録』皇別 和泉国 登美首条等。</ref> | father = {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}} | Position/Office = }}
'''Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto''' (豊城入彦命) was an ancient Japanese {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
He was a son of the 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} and is believed to be the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} refer to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto" or "Toyoki no Mikoto," while the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} refers to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto."
== Genealogy ==
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. His mother was {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}}, the daughter of Arakawatobime. He was the elder half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} and the full brother of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}.
== Records ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The {{ill|Futagoyama Kofun|ja|総社二子山古墳}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, and many others listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}.
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}.

{{Infobox royalty | name = 豊城入彦命 | Name in Various Languages = | Family Name/Title = | image = | image Size = | image Description = | Relationship = 第10代{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} | Title = | Full Name = | Rank = | Honorific = | Seal/Emblem = | Date of Birth = | Place of Birth = | Date of Death = | Place of Death = | Burial Date = | Burial Place = | Spouse 1 = | issue = {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田">『新撰姓氏録』皇別 和泉国 登美首条等。</ref> | father = {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}} | Position/Office = }}
'''Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto''' (豊城入彦命) was an ancient Japanese {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
He was a son of the 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} and is believed to be the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} refer to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto" or "Toyoki no Mikoto," while the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} refers to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto."
== Genealogy ==
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. His mother was {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}}, the daughter of Arakawatobime. He was the elder half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} and the full brother of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}.
== Records ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The {{ill|Futagoyama Kofun|ja|総社二子山古墳}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, and many others listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}.
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}.
== Notes == {{Notelist}} {{脚注ヘルプ}}
== References == {{reflist|group="原"}} {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|author=吉川弘文館|year=2010|chapter=豊城入彦命|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|isbn=978-4642014588}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futaarayama Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}

{{Infobox royalty | name = 豊城入彦命 | Name in Various Languages = | Family Name/Title = | image = | image Size = | image Description = | Relationship = 第10代{{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} | Title = | Full Name = | Rank = | Honorific = | Seal/Emblem = | Date of Birth = | Place of Birth = | Date of Death = | Place of Death = | Burial Date = | Burial Place = | Spouse 1 = | issue = {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田">『新撰姓氏録』皇別 和泉国 登美首条等。</ref> | father = {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} | mother = {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}} | Position/Office = }}
'''Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto''' (豊城入彦命) was an ancient Japanese {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince}} recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}.
He was a son of the 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}} and is believed to be the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}. The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}} refer to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto" or "Toyoki no Mikoto," while the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} refers to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto."
== Genealogy ==
He was the son of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}. His mother was {{ill|Totsu Ayumemaguwashihime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛}}, the daughter of Arakawatobime. He was the elder half-brother of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} and the full brother of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime}}.
== Records ==
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|lt=Chronicles of Japan|ja|日本書紀}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48年正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The {{ill|Futagoyama Kofun|ja|総社二子山古墳}} in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55年2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}}, and many others listed in the {{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}.
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}}.
