revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊城入彦命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Yatsunata|qid=Q15275406}}|father={{ill|Sujin|qid=Q333530}}|mother={{ill|Q97539597|qid=Q97539597}}|relatives={{ill|Prince Yamatohiko|qid=Q11387083}}; {{ill|Princess Chichitsukuyamatohime|qid=Q10904824}}; {{ill|Princess Kunikatahime|qid=Q10926851}}; {{ill|Princess Nunakiirihime|qid=Q11561867}}; {{ill|Princess Toochiniirihime|qid=Q10904619}}; {{ill|Suinin|qid=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}; {{ill|Toyosukiirihime no mikoto|qid=Q10532771}}; {{ill|Yasaka no Irihiko no Mikoto|qid=Q14759414}}; {{ill|Yasakairihime no mikoto|qid=Q10892048}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11633375]] Wikidata name: Prince Toyokiirihiko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto | Name in Various Languages = 豊城入彦命 | Family Name/Title = | image = | image Size = | image Description = | Relationship = 第10代[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | Title = | Full Name = | Rank = | Honorific = | Seal/Emblem = | Date of Birth = | Place of Birth = | Date of Death = | Place of Death = | Burial Date = | Burial Place = | Spouse 1 = | issue = {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakutsunada|qq=}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田">『新撰姓氏録』皇別 和泉国 登美首条等。</ref> | father = [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | mother = {{ill|Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|qq=}} | Position/Office = }}
'''Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto''' (豊城入彦命) was an ancient Japanese {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
He was a son of the 10th [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] and is believed to be the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}. The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]] refer to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto" or "Toyoki no Mikoto," while the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] refers to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto."
His sister is the similarly named {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}
== Genealogy ==
He was the son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. His mother was {{ill|Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|qq=}}, the daughter of Arakawatobime. He was the elder half-brother of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the full brother of {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}.
== Records ==
According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48th year 正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The [[:en:Sōja Futagoyama Kofun|Sōja Futagoyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakutsunada|qq=}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55th year 2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratabe|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|qq=}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawake|qq=}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and many others listed in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]].
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shogun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}.
== Notes == {{Notelist}}
== References == {{reflist|group="原"}} {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|author=吉川弘文館|year=2010|chapter=豊城入彦命|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|isbn=978-4642014588}} == See Also == * [[:en:Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]] * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}

'''Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto''' (豊城入彦命) was an ancient Japanese {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
He was a son of the 10th [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] and is believed to be the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}. The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]] refer to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto" or "Toyoki no Mikoto," while the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] refers to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto."
His sister is the similarly named {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}
== Genealogy ==
He was the son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. His mother was {{ill|Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|qq=}}, the daughter of Arakawatobime. He was the elder half-brother of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the full brother of {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}.
== Records ==
According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48th year 正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The [[:en:Sōja Futagoyama Kofun|Sōja Futagoyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakutsunada|qq=}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55th year 2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratabe|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|qq=}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawake|qq=}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and many others listed in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]].
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shogun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}.

According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48th year 正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The [[:en:Sōja Futagoyama Kofun|Sōja Futagoyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakutsunada|qq=}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55th year 2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratabe|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|qq=}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawake|qq=}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and many others listed in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]].
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shogun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}.

'''Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto''' (豊城入彦命) was an ancient Japanese {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
He was a son of the 10th [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] and is believed to be the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}. The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]] refer to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto" or "Toyoki no Mikoto," while the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] refers to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto."
His sister is the similarly named {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}
== Genealogy ==
He was the son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. His mother was {{ill|Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|qq=}}, the daughter of Arakawatobime. He was the elder half-brother of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the full brother of {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}.
== Records ==
According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48th year 正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The [[:en:Sōja Futagoyama Kofun|Sōja Futagoyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakutsunada|qq=}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55th year 2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratabe|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|qq=}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawake|qq=}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and many others listed in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]].
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shogun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊城入彦命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Yatsunata|WD=Q15275406}}|father={{ill|Sujin|WD=Q333530}}|mother={{ill|Q97539597|WD=Q97539597}}|relatives={{ill|Prince Yamatohiko|WD=Q11387083}}; {{ill|Princess Chichitsukuyamatohime|WD=Q10904824}}; {{ill|Princess Kunikatahime|WD=Q10926851}}; {{ill|Princess Nunakiirihime|WD=Q11561867}}; {{ill|Princess Toochiniirihime|WD=Q10904619}}; {{ill|Suinin|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}; {{ill|Toyosukiirihime no mikoto|WD=Q10532771}}; {{ill|Yasaka no Irihiko no Mikoto|WD=Q14759414}}; {{ill|Yasakairihime no mikoto|WD=Q10892048}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11633375]] Wikidata name: Prince Toyokiirihiko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto | Name in Various Languages = 豊城入彦命 | Family Name/Title = | image = | image Size = | image Description = | Relationship = 第10代[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | Title = | Full Name = | Rank = | Honorific = | Seal/Emblem = | Date of Birth = | Place of Birth = | Date of Death = | Place of Death = | Burial Date = | Burial Place = | Spouse 1 = | issue = {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakutsunada|qq=}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田">『新撰姓氏録』皇別 和泉国 登美首条等。</ref> | father = [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | mother = {{ill|Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|qq=}} | Position/Office = }}
'''Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto''' (豊城入彦命) was an ancient Japanese {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
He was a son of the 10th [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] and is believed to be the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}. The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]] refer to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto" or "Toyoki no Mikoto," while the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] refers to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto."
His sister is the similarly named {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}
== Genealogy ==
He was the son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. His mother was {{ill|Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|qq=}}, the daughter of Arakawatobime. He was the elder half-brother of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the full brother of {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}.
== Records ==
According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48th year 正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The [[:en:Sōja Futagoyama Kofun|Sōja Futagoyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakutsunada|qq=}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55th year 2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratabe|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|qq=}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawake|qq=}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and many others listed in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]].
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shogun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}.

The [[:en:Sōja Futagoyama Kofun|Sōja Futagoyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakutsunada|qq=}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55th year 2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratabe|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|qq=}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawake|qq=}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and many others listed in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]].
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shogun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊城入彦命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Yatsunata|WD=Q15275406}}|father={{ill|Sujin|WD=Q333530}}|mother={{ill|Q97539597|WD=Q97539597}}|relatives={{ill|Prince Yamatohiko|WD=Q11387083}}; {{ill|Princess Chichitsukuyamatohime|WD=Q10904824}}; {{ill|Princess Kunikatahime|WD=Q10926851}}; {{ill|Princess Nunakiirihime|WD=Q11561867}}; {{ill|Princess Toochiniirihime|WD=Q10904619}}; {{ill|Suinin|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}; {{ill|Toyosukiirihime no mikoto|WD=Q10532771}}; {{ill|Yasaka no Irihiko no Mikoto|WD=Q14759414}}; {{ill|Yasakairihime no mikoto|WD=Q10892048}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11633375]] Wikidata name: Prince Toyokiirihiko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto | Name in Various Languages = 豊城入彦命 | Family Name/Title = | image = | image Size = | image Description = | Relationship = 第10代[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | Title = | Full Name = | Rank = | Honorific = | Seal/Emblem = | Date of Birth = | Place of Birth = | Date of Death = | Place of Death = | Burial Date = | Burial Place = | Spouse 1 = | issue = {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakutsunada|qq=}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田">『新撰姓氏録』皇別 和泉国 登美首条等。</ref> | father = [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | mother = {{ill|Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|qq=}} | Position/Office = }}
'''Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto''' (豊城入彦命) was an ancient Japanese {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
He was a son of the 10th [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] and is believed to be the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}. The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]] refer to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto" or "Toyoki no Mikoto," while the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] refers to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto."
His sister is the similarly named {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}
== Genealogy ==
He was the son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. His mother was {{ill|Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|qq=}}, the daughter of Arakawatobime. He was the elder half-brother of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the full brother of {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}.
== Records ==
According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48th year 正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The [[:en:Sōja Futagoyama Kofun|Sōja Futagoyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakutsunada|qq=}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55th year 2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratabe|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|qq=}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawake|qq=}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and many others listed in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]].
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shogun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊城入彦命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Yatsunata|WD=Q15275406}}|father={{ill|Sujin|WD=Q333530}}|mother={{ill|Q97539597|WD=Q97539597}}|relatives={{ill|Prince Yamatohiko|WD=Q11387083}}; {{ill|Princess Chichitsukuyamatohime|WD=Q10904824}}; {{ill|Princess Kunikatahime|WD=Q10926851}}; {{ill|Princess Nunakiirihime|WD=Q11561867}}; {{ill|Princess Toochiniirihime|WD=Q10904619}}; {{ill|Suinin|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}; {{ill|Toyosukiirihime no mikoto|WD=Q10532771}}; {{ill|Yasaka no Irihiko no Mikoto|WD=Q14759414}}; {{ill|Yasakairihime no mikoto|WD=Q10892048}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11633375]] Wikidata name: Prince Toyokiirihiko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto | Name in Various Languages = 豊城入彦命 | Family Name/Title = | image = | image Size = | image Description = | Relationship = 第10代[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | Title = | Full Name = | Rank = | Honorific = | Seal/Emblem = | Date of Birth = | Place of Birth = | Date of Death = | Place of Death = | Burial Date = | Burial Place = | Spouse 1 = | issue = {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakutsunada|qq=}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田">『新撰姓氏録』皇別 和泉国 登美首条等。</ref> | father = [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | mother = {{ill|Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|qq=}} | Position/Office = }}
'''Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto''' (豊城入彦命) was an ancient Japanese {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
He was a son of the 10th [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] and is believed to be the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}. The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]] refer to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto" or "Toyoki no Mikoto," while the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] refers to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto."
His sister is the similarly named {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}
== Genealogy ==
He was the son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. His mother was {{ill|Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|qq=}}, the daughter of Arakawatobime. He was the elder half-brother of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the full brother of {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}.
== Records ==
According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48th year 正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The [[:en:Sōja Futagoyama Kofun|Sōja Futagoyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakutsunada|qq=}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55th year 2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratabe|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|qq=}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawake|qq=}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and many others listed in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]].
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shogun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}.
== Notes == {{Notelist}}
== References == {{reflist|group="原"}} {{reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|author=吉川弘文館|year=2010|chapter=豊城入彦命|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|isbn=978-4642014588}} == See Also == * [[:en:Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]] * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}

== Records ==
According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48th year 正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The [[:en:Sōja Futagoyama Kofun|Sōja Futagoyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakutsunada|qq=}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55th year 2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratabe|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|qq=}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawake|qq=}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and many others listed in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]].
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shogun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}.
== Notes == {{Notelist}}
== References == {{reflist|group="原"}} {{reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊城入彦命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Yatsunata|WD=Q15275406}}|father={{ill|Sujin|WD=Q333530}}|mother={{ill|Q97539597|WD=Q97539597}}|relatives={{ill|Prince Yamatohiko|WD=Q11387083}}; {{ill|Princess Chichitsukuyamatohime|WD=Q10904824}}; {{ill|Princess Kunikatahime|WD=Q10926851}}; {{ill|Princess Nunakiirihime|WD=Q11561867}}; {{ill|Princess Toochiniirihime|WD=Q10904619}}; {{ill|Suinin|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}; {{ill|Toyosukiirihime no mikoto|WD=Q10532771}}; {{ill|Yasaka no Irihiko no Mikoto|WD=Q14759414}}; {{ill|Yasakairihime no mikoto|WD=Q10892048}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11633375]] Wikidata name: Prince Toyokiirihiko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto | Name in Various Languages = 豊城入彦命 | Family Name/Title = | image = | image Size = | image Description = | Relationship = 第10代[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | Title = | Full Name = | Rank = | Honorific = | Seal/Emblem = | Date of Birth = | Place of Birth = | Date of Death = | Place of Death = | Burial Date = | Burial Place = | Spouse 1 = | issue = {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakutsunada|qq=}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田">『新撰姓氏録』皇別 和泉国 登美首条等。</ref> | father = [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | mother = {{ill|Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|qq=}} | Position/Office = }}
'''Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto''' (豊城入彦命) was an ancient Japanese {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
He was a son of the 10th [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] and is believed to be the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}. The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]] refer to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto" or "Toyoki no Mikoto," while the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] refers to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto."
His sister is the similarly named {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}
== Genealogy ==
He was the son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. His mother was {{ill|Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|qq=}}, the daughter of Arakawatobime. He was the elder half-brother of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the full brother of {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}.
== Records ==
According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48th year 正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The [[:en:Sōja Futagoyama Kofun|Sōja Futagoyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakutsunada|qq=}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55th year 2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratabe|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|qq=}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawake|qq=}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and many others listed in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]].
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shogun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊城入彦命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Yatsunata|WD=Q15275406}}|father={{ill|Sujin|WD=Q333530}}|mother={{ill|Q97539597|WD=Q97539597}}|relatives={{ill|Prince Yamatohiko|WD=Q11387083}}; {{ill|Princess Chichitsukuyamatohime|WD=Q10904824}}; {{ill|Princess Kunikatahime|WD=Q10926851}}; {{ill|Princess Nunakiirihime|WD=Q11561867}}; {{ill|Princess Toochiniirihime|WD=Q10904619}}; {{ill|Suinin|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}; {{ill|Toyosukiirihime no mikoto|WD=Q10532771}}; {{ill|Yasaka no Irihiko no Mikoto|WD=Q14759414}}; {{ill|Yasakairihime no mikoto|WD=Q10892048}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11633375]] Wikidata name: Prince Toyokiirihiko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto | Name in Various Languages = 豊城入彦命 | Family Name/Title = | image = | image Size = | image Description = | Relationship = 第10代[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | Title = | Full Name = | Rank = | Honorific = | Seal/Emblem = | Date of Birth = | Place of Birth = | Date of Death = | Place of Death = | Burial Date = | Burial Place = | Spouse 1 = | issue = {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakutsunada|qq=}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田">『新撰姓氏録』皇別 和泉国 登美首条等。</ref> | father = [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | mother = {{ill|Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|qq=}} | Position/Office = }}
'''Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto''' (豊城入彦命) was an ancient Japanese {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
He was a son of the 10th [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] and is believed to be the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}. The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]] refer to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto" or "Toyoki no Mikoto," while the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] refers to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto."
His sister is the similarly named {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}
== Genealogy ==
He was the son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. His mother was {{ill|Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|qq=}}, the daughter of Arakawatobime. He was the elder half-brother of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the full brother of {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}.
== Records ==
According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48th year 正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The [[:en:Sōja Futagoyama Kofun|Sōja Futagoyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakutsunada|qq=}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55th year 2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratabe|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|qq=}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawake|qq=}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and many others listed in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]].
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shogun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}.

The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The [[:en:Sōja Futagoyama Kofun|Sōja Futagoyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakutsunada|qq=}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55th year 2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratabe|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|qq=}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawake|qq=}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and many others listed in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]].
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shogun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊城入彦命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊城入彦命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊城入彦命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Yatsunata|WD=Q15275406}}|father={{ill|Sujin|WD=Q333530}}|mother={{ill|Q97539597|WD=Q97539597}}|relatives={{ill|Prince Yamatohiko|WD=Q11387083}}; {{ill|Princess Chichitsukuyamatohime|WD=Q10904824}}; {{ill|Princess Kunikatahime|WD=Q10926851}}; {{ill|Princess Nunakiirihime|WD=Q11561867}}; {{ill|Princess Toochiniirihime|WD=Q10904619}}; {{ill|Suinin|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}; {{ill|Toyosukiirihime no mikoto|WD=Q10532771}}; {{ill|Yasaka no Irihiko no Mikoto|WD=Q14759414}}; {{ill|Yasakairihime no mikoto|WD=Q10892048}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11633375]] Wikidata name: Prince Toyokiirihiko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto | Name in Various Languages = 豊城入彦命 | Family Name/Title = | image = | image Size = | image Description = | Relationship = 第10代[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | Title = | Full Name = | Rank = | Honorific = | Seal/Emblem = | Date of Birth = | Place of Birth = | Date of Death = | Place of Death = | Burial Date = | Burial Place = | Spouse 1 = | issue = {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakutsunada|qq=}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田">『新撰姓氏録』皇別 和泉国 登美首条等。</ref> | father = [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | mother = {{ill|Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|qq=}} | Position/Office = }}
'''Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto''' (豊城入彦命) was an ancient Japanese {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
He was a son of the 10th [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] and is believed to be the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}. The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]] refer to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto" or "Toyoki no Mikoto," while the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] refers to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto."
His sister is the similarly named {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}
== Genealogy ==
He was the son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. His mother was {{ill|Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|qq=}}, the daughter of Arakawatobime. He was the elder half-brother of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the full brother of {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}.
== Records ==
According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48th year 正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The [[:en:Sōja Futagoyama Kofun|Sōja Futagoyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakutsunada|qq=}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55th year 2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratabe|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|qq=}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawake|qq=}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and many others listed in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]].
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shogun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊城入彦命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊城入彦命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊城入彦命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Yatsunata|WD=Q15275406}}|father={{ill|Sujin|WD=Q333530}}|mother={{ill|Q97539597|WD=Q97539597}}|relatives={{ill|Prince Yamatohiko|WD=Q11387083}}; {{ill|Princess Chichitsukuyamatohime|WD=Q10904824}}; {{ill|Princess Kunikatahime|WD=Q10926851}}; {{ill|Princess Nunakiirihime|WD=Q11561867}}; {{ill|Princess Toochiniirihime|WD=Q10904619}}; {{ill|Suinin|WD=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}; {{ill|Toyosukiirihime no mikoto|WD=Q10532771}}; {{ill|Yasaka no Irihiko no Mikoto|WD=Q14759414}}; {{ill|Yasakairihime no mikoto|WD=Q10892048}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11633375]] Wikidata name: Prince Toyokiirihiko [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox royalty | name = Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto | Name in Various Languages = 豊城入彦命 | Family Name/Title = | image = | image Size = | image Description = | Relationship = 第10代[[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]{{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | Title = | Full Name = | Rank = | Honorific = | Seal/Emblem = | Date of Birth = | Place of Birth = | Date of Death = | Place of Death = | Burial Date = | Burial Place = | Spouse 1 = | issue = {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakutsunada|qq=}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田">『新撰姓氏録』皇別 和泉国 登美首条等。</ref> | father = [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | mother = {{ill|Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|qq=}} | Position/Office = }}
'''Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto''' (豊城入彦命) was an ancient Japanese {{ill|Imperial Prince|ja|皇子|vi|Hoàng tử|zh|皇子|ko|황자|lt=Imperial Prince|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Prince|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} recorded in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]].
He was a son of the 10th [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] and is believed to be the ancestor of the {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}. The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]] refer to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto" or "Toyoki no Mikoto," while the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] refers to him as "Toyoki-Irihiko no Mikoto."
His sister is the similarly named {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}
== Genealogy ==
He was the son of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. His mother was {{ill|Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|ja|遠津年魚眼眼妙媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtsu Ayume Maguwashi Hime|qq=}}, the daughter of Arakawatobime. He was the elder half-brother of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] and the full brother of {{ill|Toyosukiiri-hime|ja|豊鍬入姫命|1=Toyosukiiri-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyosukiiri-hime|qq=}}.
== Records ==
According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Chronicles of Japan]]<ref group="原">『日本書紀』崇神天皇48th year 正月戊子（10日）条。</ref>, in the 48th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, the Emperor instructed Toyoki no Mikoto and Ikume no Mikoto (later Emperor Suinin) to each have a dream to determine the successor. Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The [[:en:Sōja Futagoyama Kofun|Sōja Futagoyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakutsunada|qq=}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55th year 2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratabe|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|qq=}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawake|qq=}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and many others listed in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]].
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shogun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}.

Toyoki no Mikoto dreamed of climbing Mount Mimoro (Mount Miwa) and waving spears and swords to the east. Ikume no Mikoto dreamed of climbing the same mountain, spreading ropes in all directions, and chasing away sparrows. Based on these dreams, it was decided that Ikume no Mikoto, who envisioned securing territories and promoting agriculture, would inherit the throne, while Toyoki no Mikoto, who wielded weapons toward the east, would be sent to govern the eastern regions.
== Burial ==
The burial site of Toyoki no Mikoto is unknown. The [[:en:Sōja Futagoyama Kofun|Sōja Futagoyama Kofun]] in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], Gunma Prefecture, was traditionally regarded as his tomb until investigations dated the tomb to the late 6th century. Other theories suggest the {{ill|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|ja|大室古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmuro Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Maebashi or the {{ill|Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja|丸山古墳 (石岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Kofun (Ishioka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Ishioka, Ibaraki Prefecture.
== Descendants ==
=== Individuals === * Son: {{ill|Yakutsunada|ja|八綱田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakutsunada|qq=}}<ref group="原" name="八綱田"/> * Grandson: {{ill|Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|ja|彦狭島王|1=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Sashima no Mikoto|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇55th year 2月条。</ref> * Great-grandson: {{ill|Mimorowake no Mikoto|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}<ref group="原">『日本書紀』景行天皇56年8月条。</ref> * Great-great-grandsons: {{ill|Aratabe|ja|荒田別|1=Aratawake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratabe|13=User:Immanuelle/Aratawake|qq=}}, {{ill|Kagawake|ja|鹿我別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawake|qq=}} (巫別)<ref group="原">『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」浮田国造条。</ref>
=== Clans === Descendant clans include {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}}, and many others listed in the [[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]].
== Theories and Analysis ==
Toyoki no Mikoto's narrative aligns with tales of royal conquests, such as the {{ill|Shido Shogun|ja|四道将軍|1=Shidō Shōgun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shido Shogun|13=User:Immanuelle/Shidō Shōgun|qq=}} expeditions. These accounts illustrate the Yamato court's extension of power to distant regions. The {{ill|Kamitsukeno clan|ja|上毛野氏|1=Kamitsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno clan|qq=}} revered him as their ancestor deity and enshrined him at {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}.
